title: Take a Screenshot
summary: Capture what is on a device screen and save or share the image.
learnerPromise: You will be able to capture a screenshot on your device, find it in your photos, and share it with someone.
whyItMatters: Screenshots are useful for saving directions, error messages, receipts, and anything else you want to remember or show someone. It is often faster than writing down what you see.
realLifeUses: You want to save a map with directions so you can view it offline
An app shows an error message and you need to ask for help fixing it
You want to keep a copy of a purchase confirmation or ticket
You found something online you want to remember or discuss with someone later
materialsNeeded: A phone, tablet, or computer with a screen
result: A screenshot image saved to your device and successfully shared via message or email.
youWillLearn: How to use the correct button combination or tool to take a screenshot on your device
How to find your screenshot in your photos or files app
How to crop or edit the screenshot if needed
How to share a screenshot through message, email, or another app
miniChallenge: Take a screenshot of anything on your screen, then send it to yourself in a message.
steps: Find something on your screen you want to capture — a message, map, or webpage
Use the correct method for your device: press the volume and power buttons together on most phones, or use a keyboard shortcut on computers
Look for a brief flash or thumbnail to confirm the screenshot was taken
Open your Photos or Files app and find the screenshot in your screenshots folder
Share the screenshot to yourself via message or email to confirm it works
completionCriteria: You can find the screenshot in your photos or files and have shared it
commonProblems: The screenshot does not save — make sure you press both buttons at the same time
Cannot find the screenshot — check a folder named 'Screenshots' in your photos or files app
The screen turns off instead — you may be holding the wrong buttons for your device model
tips: On most Android phones: press Power + Volume Down at the same time
On most iPhones: press the Side button + Volume Up (or Home button + Power on older models)
On Windows: press Windows key + Shift + S for a partial screenshot
On Mac: press Command + Shift + 4 to select an area, or Command + Shift + 3 for the full screen
